summary: The IoM Companies Registry maintains a public register for 1931 Act companies. A current register record (name, number, type, status, incorporation date, registered office, directors/secretary) is publicly searchable and a Certificate of Incorporation and filed documents can be ordered. There is no single German-style certified consolidated extract, but the official public register record is the functional equivalent.

evidence.note: Free basic company search plus orderable documents (Registration Certificate, First Directors/Secretary & Registered Office, Memorandum/Articles). Equivalence inferred from the public register service.

caveats: INFERENCE: IoM issues a company-details search/certificate, not a single certified consolidated extract like a German Handelsregisterauszug; the public register record is treated as the equivalent.

summary: A 1931 Act company with a share capital must, in its annual return, list all members and the shares each holds, and must forward a signed copy to the Registrar within one month of its return date. The annual return is therefore a public, filed document evidencing shareholders. It is a point-in-time snapshot (up to ~12 months old), not a live register.

evidence.quote: The annual return of a company having a share capital shall contain a list of all persons who, at the date of the return, are members of the company, and of all persons who have ceased to be members since the date of the last return
evidence.note: s107(2) adds names, addresses and number of shares held by each existing member.

evidence.quote: the company must, within one month after that date, forward to the Department a copy signed by a director or by the manager or by the secretary of the company
evidence.note: Confirms the members-listing annual return is filed with the Registrar and thus publicly obtainable.

caveats: The 1931 annual return lists members and IS public and filed — the opposite of the 2006 Act regime.
Point-in-time: reflects members at the return date, not necessarily current holders.
Does not apply to a company limited by guarantee (s107(6)); the members-list requirement is for share-capital companies.

summary: A 1931 Act company files its memorandum and articles of association at registration; these are held by the Companies Registry and are publicly orderable as documents.

evidence.basis: IoM Companies Registry - 1931 Act Companies (official gov.im)
evidence.note: Registry document set for 1931 Act companies includes Memorandum and Articles of Association, which are filed and publicly orderable.

caveats: Verbatim statutory filing clause not quoted here; confirmed via the official registry document list (memorandum and articles are on the public file).

summary: The IoM Companies Registry maintains a public register for 2006 Act companies. A current register record (name, number, type, status, incorporation date, registered office, registered agent) is publicly searchable, and a Certificate of Incorporation and filed documents can be ordered. Same equivalence caveat as for 1931 Act: no single certified consolidated German-style extract.

evidence.note: Public company search and orderable documents. Equivalence to a Registerauszug inferred from the public register service, not from a dedicated statutory 'extract' provision.

caveats: INFERENCE: functional equivalent only; IoM has no single certified consolidated extract.
A 2006 Act register record shows the registered agent and directors only if directors are voluntarily filed (s204); it does NOT show shareholders.

documentLocal: register of members (kept by registered agent, not filed)
summary: This is the decisive distinction. Under the 2006 Act the register of members is kept at the office of the registered agent (s62, s78), is NOT filed with the Registrar, and the annual return (s85) contains only 'such particulars as may be prescribed' — it does NOT list members. Filing a copy of the register of members with the Registrar is purely OPTIONAL under s203. Therefore, by default, a current shareholder document is NOT obtainable from the registry and shareholders are NOT public. A document is only obtainable if the specific company has voluntarily elected under s203 to file its register of members.

evidence.quote: A company shall cause to be kept a register of members containing ... the names and business or residential addresses of the persons who hold shares in the company
evidence.note: Kept by the company, not filed with the Registrar.

evidence.quote: A company shall keep the following documents at the office of its registered agent -- ... the register of members maintained under section 62 or a copy of the register of members
evidence.note: Confirms register of members is held privately by the registered agent, not the Registrar.

evidence.quote: An annual return shall be made by a company containing such particulars as may be prescribed.
evidence.note: Unlike the 1931 Act s107, the 2006 annual return does NOT statutorily list members.

evidence.quote: A company may elect to file a copy of its register of members for registration by the Registrar.
evidence.note: Filing is voluntary ('may elect'); absent this election, shareholders are not on the public register.

caveats: CRUX: 2006 Act shareholders are NOT public by default — register of members kept by the registered agent and NOT filed; the annual return does not list members. This is the exact opposite of the 1931 Act regime.

CONDITIONAL EXCEPTION: if the specific company made an optional s203 election to file (and keep current) its register of members, a shareholder document IS then obtainable — rare, must be checked per company.
Optional director filing (s204) is separate and does not evidence shareholders.

summary: Incorporation of a 2006 Act company requires filing a memorandum (s2(1)(a), s5), which is on the public register. Articles are filed only if they differ from the relevant model articles (or for PCCs / unlimited-without-shares companies) per s2(1)(b); where the company adopts model articles, no bespoke articles are on file (the model articles apply by operation of law and are public). The constitution is therefore obtainable from the registry.

evidence.quote: application may be made to the Registrar for the incorporation of a company by filing -- (a) a memorandum complying with section 5; and (b) if the articles of the company are to differ from the relevant model articles ... articles.
evidence.note: Memorandum always filed and public; bespoke articles filed only where they differ from model articles.

caveats: If the company uses the statutory model articles, no separate articles document is on the registry file — the model articles apply by law rather than as a filed instrument.
Amendments to memorandum/articles must be filed (s9), so the current constitution should be reconstructable from the file.
